title: Gazpacho
description: My Thermomix take on José Andrés' silky Spanish gazpacho — a cold soup blitzed in seconds.
intro: No cooking at all — just ripe summer veg blitzed until glassy and smooth. The Thermomix turns a handful of tomatoes into the silkiest cold soup.
steps: Place the tomatoes, cucumber, capsicum, garlic, vinegar, bread and salt in the mixing bowl. Blend 30 sec / speed 7.
With the blades running on speed 5, drizzle the olive oil through the lid, then blend 1 min / speed 9 until completely silky.
Chill at least 1 hour. Check the seasoning and serve cold with an extra drizzle of olive oil
tips: The best tomatoes you can find make the whole dish.
For an ultra-smooth finish, pass it through a sieve.

faq.a: No — it's a raw, cold soup. The Thermomix just blends it silky.
It gives the soup body and that classic velvety texture.

faq.q: Is this the original José Andrés recipe?
faq.a: No. This is Chef Aly’s independent Thermomix reinterpretation inspired by José Andrés and gazpacho; it is not endorsed by or affiliated with the chef.
